findings.message: DeleteButton performs an irreversible destructive action with no confirmation step. A misclick immediately fires the DELETE request and reloads the page; there is no way for the user to recover.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation guard before the fetch, e.g. `if (!window.confirm('Delete this item?')) return;`, or replace the browser confirm with an inline confirmation UI (e.g. a two-step 'Are you sure?' flow).

findings.message: No loading or disabled-while-pending state: the button stays interactive while the DELETE request is in flight, allowing double-submits. There is also no success or error feedback — if the request fails the page silently reloads as if it succeeded.
findings.suggestion: Track a `isPending` state, disable the button and show a spinner/label change while pending, and surface an error message (e.g. via a caught rejection) if the request fails rather than unconditionally calling `window.location.reload()`.
